prose: Fukui reactivity indices were computed for benzaldehyde (14 atoms) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral singlet). Exact four-centre integrals were used (density fitting was NOT enabled). Mulliken population analysis was used for the partial charges on the neutral (N), cation (N−1, charge +1, multiplicity 2), and anion (N+1, charge −1, multiplicity 2) species. All three SCF calculations converged. Integrity status: ok; trustworthy: true. Charge conservation checks passed: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000. No warnings were reported. The headline value reported is the maximum f⁺ (most electrophilic site): f⁺ = 0.39938964 at atom 3 (C). The most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (O, the carbonyl oxygen) with f⁻ = 0.23839906, consistent with benzaldehyde's known lone-pair donor character. Command: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui [input.xyz] --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1. Output: benzaldehyde_fukui_dft.json. Plot: .../agent_call_01_plot.png.
